result_summary: S1-S9 passed with 10-10 seeds per gate; held-out splits and paired intervals are encoded where required.
paper_implication: The Methods and Results can present the synthetic hierarchy as verified software and mathematical validation.
boundary: This does not establish calibration on nonstationary real detector noise.

result_summary: The latest archived remote run passed Stage 0 and recorded environment, dependency, test, table, and figure logs.
paper_implication: The reproducibility statement can say the validation workflow was exercised on a clean remote Linux checkout.
boundary: Reproducibility is separate from scientific acceptance of the real-data gate.

result_summary: Official H1/L1 DATA coverage was present; H1: 62/62 valid, L1: 62/62 valid; raw/evidence checksums verified.
paper_implication: The paper can state that the 256-second GW150914-centered analysis interval had valid H1/L1 off-source and event windows.
boundary: The raw strain arrays are not stored in this bundle; the bundle stores metadata, checksums, and derived evidence.

result_summary: Repository/GWpy PSD ratios have median, p05, and p95 equal to 1.0 at recorded precision; relative L2 error is 0.0. Exact PSD match recorded: True.
paper_implication: The PSD-estimation method can be described as independently verified against GWpy for identical calibration windows.
boundary: PSD estimator agreement does not prove that the resulting matched statistic is calibrated on held-out real noise.

result_summary: H1 random median/min/max 1.986/1.396/7.166; L1 random 1.263/1.209/3.093. H1 chronological median 3.183; L1 chronological median 2.964.
paper_implication: The Results should report this as a negative real-data result: global PSD calibration failed despite PSD reference agreement.
boundary: Do not convert event or injection scores into significance or sensitivity claims under this failed calibration.

result_summary: Passed the predeclared shared-statistic implementation identity gate: synthetic max abs 4.98e-15, max relative L2 1.47e-15; H1 max abs 1.6e-12, max relative L2 1.7e-13; L1 max abs 1.45e-12, max relative L2 3.04e-13.
paper_implication: The Results can state that explicit FFT convolution and GWpy convolution agree when forced to use the same FIR coefficients and normalization.
boundary: This does not prove that the original PSD-domain GLS statistic and a finite-duration FIR statistic are mathematically equivalent; that comparison remains diagnostic sensitivity.

result_summary: Synthetic primary local-64 control passed (std 1.148); real H1/L1 failed the [0.8, 1.2] primary gate with full coverage (H1 global/local-64 std 1.771/9.424; L1 global/local-64 std 7.288/8.827).
paper_implication: The Results should report this as a verified negative real-data follow-up: the tested local PSD model did not calibrate this GWOSC interval.
boundary: The failure does not invalidate the synthetic theory; it shows that this real interval is not calibrated by the tested global or fixed-radius local PSD models. Do not retune radius, cuts, thresholds, or windows after inspection.
